codeReview.summary: Solid implementation covering all expected behaviors. Init loads test.png from 9p into the shared font+image buffer, sends MSG_IMAGE_CONFIG to compositor. Compositor decodes PNG via drawing::png_decode, stores decoded BGRA pixels in heap-allocated Vec, renders centered/clipped image in content area via blit_blend. Status bar correctly switches to 'Image viewer | WxH px' format. Chrome (title bar, status bar, drop shadows) remains visible. Error handling is present for missing/corrupted PNG data (graceful fallback to text editor mode). 4 new tests cover clipping (opaque and alpha-blended blit), chrome region protection, and PNG decode color correctness. Message ordering is correct (both config messages sent before compositor starts). Buffer sizing for decode is safe (overestimates for non-RGBA color types).

issues.description: png_decode_to_surface_correct_colors silently passes if test.png is missing (uses `if let Ok(data)`). This matches the existing pattern in png_decode_test_image_from_file above, but means the test provides no coverage guarantee when the file is absent. Consider at minimum a println noting the skip.

First png_decode call uses hardcoded buffer size `128 * 128 * 4 + 128` that assumes test.png is 128x128. If the test image changes dimensions, this allocation could be too small (causing decode failure) or the test would silently skip. Using png_header() first (as the compositor does) would be more robust.

decode_buf Vec (~65KB for 128x128) is allocated then copied to image_pixels Vec (~65KB), doubling transient heap usage. With a bump allocator, decode_buf's memory is never reclaimed. For the current 128x128 test image this is negligible, but for larger images the waste would compound. A future optimization could decode directly into the final buffer.

sharedStateObservations.observation: architecture.md's Font Pipeline section says 'Both fonts loaded from system/share/ via 9p into a single 256 KiB shared buffer (mono at offset 0, prop at offset mono_len).' This is now stale — the buffer also holds PNG image data at offset mono_len + prop_len. The init tuple changed from (pa, mono_len, prop_len) to (pa, mono_len, prop_len, png_offset, png_len).

summary: Image viewer feature passes review. The implementation correctly loads PNG data from 9p, decodes it in the compositor, displays it centered/clipped in the content area with chrome visible alongside, and shows image dimensions in the status bar. Error paths gracefully fall back to text editor mode. 4 new tests cover the key behaviors. Three non-blocking issues noted: test silently skips when test.png is missing, hardcoded buffer size assumption in one test, and transient double-allocation during decode. One shared state observation: architecture.md's font pipeline description is now stale (buffer also contains PNG data).
